---
title: "lab3"
format: html
---

```{r}
# Load required packages
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(here)
library(lubridate)
library(tidycensus)
library(tigris)

# Optional (for cleaner model outputs later)
library(modelsummary)

philly_sales <- st_read(("data/opa_properties_public.geojson"))

philly_sales <- philly_sales %>%
  mutate(
    sale_date = as.Date(sale_date)
  )

summary(philly_sales)


# FILTER the dataset for all observations where sale date is 2023-2024, sale price is greater than 10,000 and there are no NAs in sale_price, number_of_bedrooms, number_of_bathrooms, or location
philly_sales_2324 <- philly_sales %>%
  filter(
    year(sale_date) %in% c(2023, 2024),
    !is.na(sale_price),
    sale_price > 10000,
    sale_price < 2000000
  )%>%
  filter(!is.na(number_of_bedrooms),
         !is.na(number_of_bathrooms),
         !is.na(location))

# FILTER for just Residential, Apartment, and Mixed use properties
### Category codes 1, 2, and 3 ignore vacant lots and special use cases such as houses of worship or domatories
philly_sales_res <- philly_sales_2324 %>%
  filter(
    category_code %in% c(1,2,3) 
  )


### Let's try a baseline model:
baseline_model <- lm(sale_price ~ total_livable_area + number_of_bathrooms, data = philly_sales_res)


summary(baseline_model)

hist(philly_sales_res$sale_price)



# NOW LET'S DOWNLOAD ACS DATA
### Including education, poverty level, median hh income, total pop

acs_vars <- c(
  # Population
  total_pop          = "B01003_001",
 
  # Median Household Income
  median_hh_income   = "B19013_001",
 
  # Poverty
  poverty_total      = "B17001_001",   # Total population for whom poverty is determined
  poverty_below      = "B17001_002",   # Population below poverty level
 
  # Degree Attainment (25+ population)
  edu_total          = "B15003_001",   # Total population 25+
  edu_bachelors      = "B15003_022",   # Bachelor's degree
  edu_masters        = "B15003_023",   # Master's degree
  edu_professional   = "B15003_024",   # Professional degree
  edu_doctorate      = "B15003_025"    # Doctorate degree
)

philly_tracts <- get_acs(
  geography = "tract",          # Changed from "block group" to "tract"
  variables = acs_vars,
  state     = "PA",
  county    = "Philadelphia",
  year      = 2024,             # Most recent 5-year ACS
  survey    = "acs5",
  geometry  = TRUE,             # Pulls sf geometry for spatial analysis
  output    = "wide"            # One row per tract
)
 
# NOW LET'S CLEAN THE ACS DATA AND DERIVE VARIABLE

philly_tracts_clean <- philly_tracts %>%
  mutate(area_km2 = as.numeric(st_area(geometry)) / 1e6,
         pop_density = total_popE / area_km2,
         poverty_rate = (poverty_belowE / poverty_totalE) * 100,
         edu_college_plus = edu_bachelorsE + edu_mastersE + edu_professionalE + edu_doctorateE,
         college_rate = (edu_college_plus / edu_totalE) * 100
      ) %>%
  select(
    GEOID, NAME, total_popE, pop_density, median_hh_incomeE, poverty_rate, college_rate, geometry
  )

glimpse(philly_tracts_clean)

### NOW LETS SAVE AS A GEOJSON

# Save as GeoJSON
st_write(philly_tracts_clean, "philly_acs_tract.geojson", delete_if_exists = TRUE)

### NOW CLEAN AND JOIN TO SALES GEOJSON

philly_tracts_clean <- st_transform(philly_tracts_clean, st_crs(philly_sales_res))

sales_with_acs <- st_join(philly_sales_res, philly_tracts_clean, join = st_within)

# NEXT LET'S DOWNLOAD AND LOAD SOME SPATIAL FEATURES (SEPTA, Parks, and EPA walkscore)
### SEPTA STOPS: https://opendataphilly.org/datasets/septa-routes-stops-locations/
### PARKS AND RECREATION PROPERTIES: https://opendataphilly.org/datasets/ppr-properties/
### EPA WALKSCORES: https://edg.epa.gov/EPADataCommons/public/OA/EPA_SmartLocationDatabase_V3_Jan_2021_Final.csv

##LETS START WITH WALKSCORES:
#Walkscore requires a paid API, so we used a free EPA alternative:
epa_walk <- read_csv("data/EPA_SmartLocationDatabase_V3_Jan_2021_Final.csv")

# Filter to Philadelphia (FIPS county code 42101)
philly_walk <- epa_walk %>%
  filter(STATEFP == 42, COUNTYFP == 101)
# Make a GEOID from scratch to match with GEOIDs in the sales_with_acs table
### Necessary because the EPA csv used scientific notation for its GEOID and lost its specificity
philly_walk_bg <- philly_walk %>%
  filter(STATEFP == 42, COUNTYFP == 101) %>%
  mutate(GEOID_BG = paste0(
    str_pad(STATEFP, 2, pad = "0"),
    str_pad(COUNTYFP, 3, pad = "0"),
    str_pad(TRACTCE, 6, pad = "0"),
    str_pad(BLKGRPCE, 1, pad = "0")   # Add block group digit
  )) %>%
  group_by(GEOID_BG) %>%
  summarise(walkability = mean(NatWalkInd, na.rm = TRUE))


# NOW LET'S DO SEPTA STOPS
SEPTA_stops <- st_read(("data/Transit_Stops_(Spring_2025).geojson"))
# st_transform to use the same projection
SEPTA_stops <- st_transform(SEPTA_stops, st_crs(sales_with_acs))

# Distance from each home to nearest SEPTA stop (in meters)
nearest_stop <- st_nearest_feature(sales_with_acs, SEPTA_stops)
sales_with_acs$dist_to_septa <- st_distance(
  sales_with_acs,
  SEPTA_stops[nearest_stop, ],
  by_element = TRUE
) %>% as.numeric()


# NOW LET'S DO PARKS DATA
parks <- st_read("data/PPR_Properties.geojson")

#make sure the CRS matches
parks <- st_transform(parks, st_crs(sales_with_acs))

#Distance from each home to nearest park (in meters)
nearest_park <- st_nearest_feature(sales_with_acs, parks)
sales_with_acs$dist_to_park <- st_distance(
  sales_with_acs,
  parks[nearest_park, ],
  by_element = TRUE
) %>% as.numeric()


# OK NOW LETS GET BLOCK GROUP BOUNDARIES SO WE CAN JOIN WALKABILITY DATA
philly_bg <- block_groups(state = "PA", county = "Philadelphia", year = 2024) %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(sales_with_acs))
#Spatial join to get block group GEOID for each home
sales_with_acs <- st_join(
  sales_with_acs,
  philly_bg %>% select(GEOID_BG = GEOID),
  join = st_within
  )

# Now let's join the walkability scores on the block group GEOID
sales_with_acs <- sales_with_acs %>%
  left_join(philly_walk_bg, by = "GEOID_BG")

#Let's just run some checks to see if anything looks off
sales_with_acs %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(sale_price, total_livable_area, number_of_bedrooms, pop_density, median_hh_incomeE, poverty_rate, college_rate, walkability, dist_to_septa, dist_to_park) %>%
  summary()

hist(sales_with_acs$sale_price)

```
